data.title: Matthews International: Activists Holster Guns Till Beyond 2028
data.description:
Matthews International (MATW) just had a development that is of relevance for shareholders with respect to the activist presence from Barington. While they remain invested, a standstill agreement is now in place that is seeing Barington withdraw its board nomination proposal and agreeing not to disrupt things for two years to let the current board play things out. Barington is also being compensated with $750k for out-of-pocket expenses related to the activist campaign, relative to $133m in AUM.

As we understand it, MATW is Barington's largest holding with a value of $21m as of the latest 13-F.

On one hand, this is an unsettling turn from the previous shareholder letters that saw the MATW board make appeals to shareholders on the basis that Barington nominees were "inexperienced", "short sighted" and whose best suggestions would have "destroyed long-term shareholder value". The current management, possibly because of the activist pressure thus far, has made exits that were very favorable for shareholders, but for a positive interpretation, one would have to think that the current management and board are now genuinely aligned. This would mean some agreement to continue to monetize assets and perhaps to seek a partnership with respect to the DBE business.

In all, the MATW profile is much more dominated by the casket business, which we believe is close to benchmark margins. Leverage should be near target by now, and other capital allocation priorities should start coming to the fore. Reduced leverage is a major factor to reduce overhang on the stock, and a clearer death industry profile should make the stock more easy to parse and more attractive. An acquisition by an outside sponsor would be great and is more possible in the lowering cost of capital environment.

A Bit More on the Agreement

Some of the key elements of the agreement are the following.

  • This Agreement shall terminate following the conclusion of the 2028 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. Upon such termination, this Agreement shall have no further force and effect.

We are currently at the 2026 meeting, where Barington is withdrawing its nominees to challenge the incumbent board. There was probably a good chance they would win considering the close race last time and the support from several proxy advisory firms.

Barington has also made general agreements where it will not mobilize other investors to oppose the current board, make shareholder proposals for the consideration of other shareholders, or disparage the current board. In the press release, which was agreed upon jointly, Barington indicates that the agreement reflects alignment on the strategic review of the company and the current direction of value creation, which has so far included the very solid exits from SGK and also the warehouse automation businesses at great multiples and with the effect of reducing leverage, which in our previous coverage we believe may be at target now.

The agreement also included a $750k reimbursement to Barington. It's quite a lot considering a $133m AUM at Barington LP if we are to trust that figure and then benchmark the payment to a standard 1% fee on that AUM. It's a relatively small cost for the MATW shareholder, but it's not that great to see money being extracted from the company by one specific shareholder to be socialized by the rest.

Two years should be enough time for the management to address outstanding issues, which are also on our agenda as investors.

  • The calendaring business for DBE had great orders that never liquidated fully due to the halt in growth of EVs in Western markets. Barington had floated in the past the need for this business to find a strategic partner to properly tackle the market, beyond just a demonstration production line and other initiatives to try to drum up interest from more marginal clients - where ostensibly it has been the auto major gigafactory orders that did not materialize properly yet for MATW so far.
  • Not being shy about courting interest for the casket business as well. MATW's memorialization business is at similar adjusted EBITDA margins to what Hillenbrand's (HI) casket business was at before it was sold to sponsors. There isn't that much space for margin improvement on that basis, but with falling costs of capital, sponsors who are coming back to private markets may be interested once again in mobilizing leverage to purchase a recession-resistant business in the death care industry.

Bottom Line and Looking Forward

On the other hand, the lack of immediate activist pressure does do away with an important catalyst, and we haven't been impressed by the current management's appeal to long-term shareholder value creation so far. It did seem that without the activist pressure thus far, the business would have remained conglomerated and would have been less aggressive in making total exits, though that is just us speculating. For example, we want to be confident that a sale of the entire memorialization business could be possible under the current management, where we do believe the market for assets like that in private sales is normalizing on lower costs of capital.

For the next quarter, we'd also be interested in what is happening with the energy segment within industrial technologies. Has there been any growth in order backlog, for example, particularly as it seems that the gigafactories in the US may not go forward? We are not expecting much performance from the battery technology business. The other effect we'd want to see in terms of earnings is if the deflation in most commodities is beginning to take hold in unit margins in memorialization, particularly as traction from previous pricing pushes seems to have held up so far.

In terms of valuation, below is an update for our crude pro forma expectations of debt and EBITDA for the various segments.

That memorialization dominates now is clear. We want to see some of the slow inventory turnover and commodity costing deliver its latent effect to unit economics, though we don't expect significant developments in DBE into Q1. We would maybe want to hear a more candid strategic comment on what is going to be done with that business if organically growing it is still an issue. In terms of the commentary, we want to see what management now says about capital allocation, particularly as the activists are now less of an immediate pressure. While deleveraging is very important, particularly as the 2.5x target leverage that we believe has been reached as the warehouse automation division sale is still quite a lot of leverage, we may want to hear about buybacks that they didn't follow up on in the past and whether shareholder payouts could be back on the menu quite soon.

Therefore, while we are still invested, we're a hold for now.

data.title: Alphabet: Q4 Earnings Surprises May Not Be Just About AI
data.description:
Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL)(GOOG) is probably the biggest AI darling of the last few quarters, particularly after Berkshire Hathaway opened a large position and the release of a groundbreaking Gemini 3 large language model. As a result, GOOGL was the best-performing "Magnificent 7" stock of 2025. The momentum remains strong as GOOGL delivered almost 30% total return since my previous coverage before the Q3 2025 earnings release. Just two weeks left before Google's Q4 earnings release, and I think that it is a good time to update my investment thesis before this crucial catalyst. I expect Google to continue its solid bull run in 2026 as well because there are various bullish factors surrounding the company. All these bullish factors significantly increase the probability of rapid multiples contraction, meaning that I reiterate a strong buy rating for this wonderful stock.

Reasons to continue buying GOOGL/GOOG

Since my previous coverage was published before the previous earnings release, I believe I must cover Q3 earnings at least briefly. It was a confident double beat, with a notably accelerated YoY revenue growth (16% up compared to 13.8% in Q2). Finally, it was another quarter with notable EPS growth, which was powered by operating leverage (gross margin expansion).

For the upcoming quarterly earnings, scheduled for February 4, revenue growth is expected to retreat slightly (a 15.4% YoY growth). However, Wall Street analysts believe that Google's EPS growth will maintain a 20%+ YoY growth rate. Analysts are optimistic about Google's ability to deliver robust Q4 earnings, as there have been many more positive earnings revisions recently compared to the number of downgrades.

I think that Wall Street's expectations about the headline numbers of Google's Q4 earnings release are reasonable. First and foremost, there is strong growth momentum both for the topline and EPS, which is obvious from the double-digit revenue growth over the last nine quarters, together with consistent bottom line growth. Analysts' forecasts look reasonable if we look at the historical sequential dynamic between Q3 and Q4 as well. We have Google's headline number over the last 20 quarters here, and we can see that QoQ revenue growth between Q3 and Q4 was historically really strong, while there were some struggles in sequential EPS growth. The same is expected for Q4 2025. Finally, Google's solid earnings surprise record means that Wall Street analysts are usually quite conservative in estimating the company's headline numbers.

Besides the earnings record, there are various fundamental factors supporting robust YoY revenue and EPS growth forecast. While Google's relatively recent success in generative AI with the Gemini 3 model release is undisputable, it is vital to keep in mind that the company's core business is digital advertising. Therefore, it should be the central topic of any Google-related analysis. Google remains the undisputed number one player in the global digital advertising business, as its Q3 digital advertising revenue was significantly higher than Meta's. Therefore, we can assess the company's core business's near-term growth prospects by focusing mostly on the macro environment. Digital advertising budgets of businesses depend on their financial well-being, which in turn depends on the macroeconomy. The latest available U.S. GDP reading, Q3 2025, was surprisingly strong with the fastest growth in two years. Given the fact that the U.S. unemployment rate remains quite stable and notably below 5%, I expect Google's advertising revenue guidance for 2026 to be quite bullish.

From the perspective of YouTube's long-term growth potential, there might be some big positive news from the management during the earnings call. During the last few days, there were reports that two large European media groups, the British BBC and the French CMA, are both close to inking landmark partnership agreements with YouTube, which will lead to a major shift toward distributing large amounts of content via the video streaming giant. If Google's management officially announces these partnerships during the earnings call, it might lead to significant upgrades to Wall Street's long-term revenue and EPS growth forecasts. Given that just before Christmas, there was an announcement that the Oscars will stream on YouTube starting from 2029, Google's video streaming platform is rapidly cementing its positioning as a major media player of the modern era. YouTube already boasts a multi-billion audience, and having the Oscars, BBC, and CMA in its corner will further boost its reach. It is a great foundation to build a cash digital advertising machine, which will be comparable to the core search engine business over the long term.

If we speak about the hottest topic of the modern era, the cloud and AI combination, there were some quite interesting partnerships here as well. For example, Google is now the official partner of Apple to power AI improvements to a number of services, including the voice assistant Siri. Having a partnership with the world's leading smartphone producer means that Google excels in AI and provides additional validation. Another interesting AI-related partnership that was inked in 2026 is with Walmart, an iconic retail giant with $700 billion in TTM sales. Under the partnership, Google will build AI agents to streamline the shopping experience for Walmart. Given Walmart's vast footprint in retail and Google's excellence in AI, it might become a highly synergistic partnership over the long term.

Furthermore, with all the fundamental positives and likely solid Q4 earnings release, it will be quite easy for GOOGL to maintain its impressive share price momentum. Investors are getting increasingly excited about GOOGL, which is obvious from the rapidly growing RSI indicator. New positive surprises from the company, which are likely to be announced during the earnings call, will certainly boost investors' confidence and increase demand for the stock.

Finally, GOOGL's valuation setup looks great. Given several positive fundamental developments related to Google's long-term revenue and EPS growth potential, an aggressive P/E ratio contraction appears to be inevitable. The company's P/E ratio is expected to dip below 30 by the end of FY2026, which looks like a compelling valuation considering the long-term potential to capitalize on YouTube and AI.

Reasons to avoid GOOGL/GOOG

In my opinion, GOOGL is very attractively valued because its highly probable continued robust EPS growth will inevitably lead to substantial multiples contraction. However, Wall Street analysts aren't as excited about Google's valuation as I am. The average target price is approximately $339, implying a tiny upside potential from current levels. On the other hand, the picture was similar when I wrote my previous bullish thesis on GOOGL. Nevertheless, the stock delivered almost a 30% return to shareholders since then.

Google is a top AI play with solid positioning in the modern digital era. However, it is not a company without relative strategic weaknesses. I am using the word "relative" here because I don't want readers to get me wrong. Google's strategic positioning is strong in terms of the entire world, but it is the weakest one compared to Amazon and Microsoft in terms of the cloud infrastructure market share. Google's third spot looks intact, given the large gap with Alibaba. However, Google is also significantly behind both Microsoft and Amazon in terms of market share. It is a significant strategic drawback because it means a smaller customer base in a rapidly growing cloud business, which means limited cross-selling potential compared to the leaders.

Finally, even stocks of a $4 trillion company like Google can experience substantial sentiment-driven selloffs. For example, last year's "Liberation Day" caused panic in the broader equity market, and GOOGL also saw a notable share price decline. Since 2026 started with various developments that can undermine the global geopolitical stability (Venezuela, Iran, Greenland), we might see new panic selloffs this year as well. And even a fundamentally strong stock like GOOGL will likely follow the broader market in this case.

The verdict

Google remains one of the top AI plays with great potential for further growth. Thus, I believe that it remains a strong buy, and I keep adding more shares to my portfolio.

data.title: IREN: 88% Upside Potential As AI Revenue Scales Toward $3.4 Billion ARR
data.description:
Investment Thesis

IREN Limited (IREN) is one of the new players in the field of cloud infrastructure for AI, offering a unique advantage in the form of existing capacity required for the deployment of data centers. Earlier, this company was valued by the market as a "proxy for Bitcoin," carrying out large-scale mining of the first cryptocurrency. But in 2026, it’s expected that IREN will fully transition to being an infrastructure player in the AI computing space, backed up by a long-term contract signed with Microsoft (MSFT). Considering investors' interest in IREN shares as a new player in the AI field, the key to maintaining the previous growth in capitalization will be achieving the Annualized Run-Rate Revenue (ARR) targets in the AI Cloud segment. IREN management expects results in the billions of dollars by the end of 2026. I would like to take a closer look at the company's growth catalysts, how it competes with other companies, as well as the risks that may arise during its transformation. Given the results, it is my opinion that IREN shares still have significant growth potential, so my recommendation for this asset is "Buy."

IREN's Strategy and Growth Drivers

From 2024, IREN Limited has been undergoing an active transformation from a conventional Bitcoin miner to an infrastructure player in the field of AI computing. Now, the company is using its capacity, formerly used for blockchain network calculations, for next-gen data center operations. Their rebranding and reorganization by the end of 2024 show that the company has picked a new growth strategy, using current trends in AI development. Their main goal is to transition to AI Cloud dominance in the company's EBITDA structure. Still, as of early 2026, we think IREN should keep splitting into two main business areas because Bitcoin mining is still the main source of EBITDA, based on data for Q1 FY2026.

As an example, if we break down IREN's revenue, the Bitcoin mining part is $232.9 million. It managed to mine 2,039 Bitcoin coins with an electricity cost of $0.046 per kWh. Meanwhile, its AI Cloud service only brought in $7.3 million, showing how small this new business is still in the overall financial results. However, by 2026, the balance of these revenue sources is expected to change, and the target margin for AI Cloud is set at around 85%.

The decision to maintain the capacity to mine Bitcoin is a sound one, since transitioning to an infrastructure player in AI requires time, during which not only new-generation data centers are deployed, but also work is carried out to establish contractual relationships with customers who will be provided with the computing power itself. Parallel to the operation of its own data centers, IREN is deploying ASIC miners. The company's competitive advantages, which include ownership of the land where its energy infrastructure facilities are located, also contribute to this diversification of its business. Another point to consider is that IREN follows a green agenda, utilizing 100% renewable energy to power its data centers. The energy is mainly generated by hydroelectric power plants and wind turbines located in British Columbia (Canada) and Texas (USA).

Nevertheless, the fourfold increase in IREN's market capitalization in 2025 would not have been possible if it had not developed a new business line in the field of AI. The company is utilizing its existing mining infrastructure to convert it into next-generation data centers. As a result, it is able to provide cloud services for AI computing. What I mean by "new generation" is IREN's data centers, using special liquid cooling (Horizon), enabling more efficient cooling of operating graphics processors, thereby increasing their operating power without the risk of overheating the equipment itself. As the main driver of IREN's stock growth in 2026, I would point to the $9.7 billion contract signed with Microsoft. Partner agreements between the companies are organized for 5 years, throughout which IREN is obliged to provide the necessary computing power based on NVIDIA (NVDA) GPUs such as H100/H200 and Blackwell in order to support the training and operation of MSFT neural networks.

Competition for IREN

Analyzing IREN's competitive environment, I would also highlight competitors in two main business areas: the Bitcoin mining sector and the AI cloud infrastructure sector. When it comes to mining, however, there are direct competitors that, like IREN, use hybrid AI miners. That is, they are the same companies that are converting their facilities into data centers. In this market segment, there are 4 main players. Core Scientific (CORZ) is the most serious competitor. Although it previously went bankrupt, CORZ managed to get out of its financial hole by reorganizing its business and has already received a $9 billion takeover bid from CoreWeave (CRWV). Nevertheless, major shareholders of the company did not approve this transaction. A competitive advantage of CORZ is that the company has enormous amounts of available electricity and currently leases 400 MW of data processing capacity to new customers.

Its competitors also include companies such as Hut 8 (HUT), undergoing a similar transformation to IREN, though at a slower pace. Also worth mentioning is TeraWulf (WULF), the competitive advantage of which is its image as an environmentally friendly company that maintains AI capacity through the use of nuclear energy. Among other competitors, one can mention Applied Digital (APLD), who were the first to begin the transformation from a traditional miner to an AI player, extending their current order portfolio to $16 billion.

There are also several companies that have been traditional Bitcoin miners to date. They are MARA Holdings (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT), and CleanSpark (CLSK). These companies are betting on hashrate, affordable energy access, or increased cryptocurrency mining. Management at IREN has chosen a different path of development, so it is more relevant to analyze competition among companies that are specialized in AI clouds. I believe that the main competitor here is Nebius (NBIS), and in a recent article about them, they discussed the reasons why their shares are recommended as a "Buy." Literally just recently, in September 2025, Nebius signed a $17.4 billion agreement with Microsoft to provide the computing power needed to run the Azure cloud application. The company was able to do this thanks to its data centers around the world. Its data center in Finland, for example, has been recognized as one of the most energy-efficient in the world. NBIS also has data center facilities in the US, Israel, and France.

But, apart from NBIS, IREN's competitors are CoreWeave and Lambda Labs. Just like NBIS, CoreWeave has the same competitive advantages, including priority access to the latest and best NVIDIA chips. In addition, the CRWV is capable of attracting large credit lines secured by the GPUs themselves. Meanwhile, Lambda is recognized as one of the most established players in the cloud infrastructure market, offering NVIDIA chips for rent and benefiting from deep integration into the developer ecosystem.

IREN's Financial Profile and Expectations for 2026

The financial profile of IREN's business is still far from ideal (despite its enormous potential for future profits), with all the Valuation Grade group's metrics still showing inflated values. But this is normal for a company that's aggressively scaling up as it enters a new, quickly growing market. In addition, based on data for Q1 FY2026, the company's revenue was $240.3 million, a 348.61% increase compared to the same period in FY2025. The figures were $11.8 million higher than the consensus forecast. Speaking of Non-GAAP EPS, it turned out to be $1.08 instead of the expected $0.14.

But for 2026, things are looking way more promising, if you ask me. Firstly, they're expecting to see the first results from the contract they signed with Microsoft. Management estimates that once everything's up and running, they'll be pulling in about $1.9 billion a year. Furthermore, they plan to significantly boost their revenue in the AI cloud segment, with a record $3.4 billion expected in 2026. For this, data center capacity is expected to keep growing, and GPUs will go from 23,000 to 140,000 units. With this in mind, back in October 2025, they brought in an extra $875 million by issuing interest-free convertible bonds (and an extra $125 million through an option for an additional buyback), which bumped up their liquidity balance to $1.8 billion. It then conducted another issue of convertible bonds in December 2025 for $2 billion with an additional option for $300 million. This money will be used not only to purchase new GPUs but also to implement its project portfolio. In April 2026, it plans to launch the Sweetwater 1 substation with a capacity of 1.4 GW. At the end of 2027, it will launch a second phase with an additional 600 MW.

Awaiting Q2 FY26 Quarterly Results

The IREN consensus forecast indicates an expected negative EPS of -0.09 dollars and revenue of 227.3 million dollars. Consensus forecasts indicate an expected negative EPS of -$0.09 and revenue of $227.33 million. Over the past three months, however, forecasts for EPS and revenue have been revised nine times, each time downward. I believe that the probability of seeing better-than-expected data is quite high, given that the last two quarterly reports came in with positive surprises. Furthermore, analysts' current consensus forecast includes concerns discussed in December 2025 about a possible slowdown in the AI supercycle. Considering the signed MSFT contract and capacity scaling, I expect IREN's actual results to be much better than initially expected by the consensus forecast of analysts.

IREN Valuation

In order to assess IREN's potential value, we must take into account that using traditional P/E multiples is not reasonable, since the company has an enormous CAPEX. As a result, other multiples should be used. An example is the EV/Sales valuation. The company's revenue forecast for 2026 is currently $3.4 billion. The EV/Sales ratio would therefore be 5.58x. The EV/Sales ratios of its main competitors are higher. NBIS, for example, based on a projected revenue of $3.45 billion for 2026, would have a multiplier of 7.86x. The market estimates CRWV at 13.14x. Taking their median, we get 10.5x. This means that if we revalue the shares to the median EV/Sales multiplier, the IREN price will go up by 88%, going from the current $55.30 to $103.96.

IREN Risks

To my mind, a big risk for IREN stock is tied to how it's changing its business. Despite signing a deal with MSFT, a few critical things still need to be considered. This is especially true if it leads to a mismatch between how fast costs and revenue are growing. The year 2026 will provide market participants with answers to questions about the profitability of the AI Cloud segment. The IREN management has set a target of 85%, an extremely high estimate, yet realistic given the technological expertise of the company's team. First and foremost, this problem of mismatch is possible due to potential delays in deploying additional infrastructure. This is because the company is planning a huge increase in capacity from 23,000 GPUs to 140,000 GPUs by the end of 2026. And any delays, such as connecting to the ERCOT grid, could push back when the new capacity starts generating revenue.

Unfortunately, we also note that IREN is becoming highly concentrated on one major client (MSFT). While this deal is a "golden ticket" that will enable the company to realize all of its ambitious plans, the company is validating its business model by making itself dependent on a single counterparty. Any problems with MSFT will lead to a sharp correction in IREN's share price. One of the reasons for this could be the same delays in scaling the cloud infrastructure for AI computing. With the focus on a single customer, there is a risk of shareholder equity dilution, as in December 2025, together with the issue of convertible bonds, there was an additional issue of 39.7 million shares at a price of $41.12, allowing the company to raise an additional $1.63 billion in capital.

Conclusion

Overall, IREN's fundamental analysis shows that its shares are really attractive for investors. One of the main reasons for IREN's continued growth will be the successful transformation of its business model, which will see AI Cloud's share of revenue jump, becoming the main business area. Taking into account the expectations from the implementation of the contract with MSFT and the achievement of a margin of 85%, it is a realistic goal. Therefore, this will allow the company to reach a new level of competitive development, enabling it to grow in value by 88% based on its EV/Sales multiples.
